req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 5
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement enforces a 7-day advance scheduling window and 2FA confirmation. Tests are derived as follows: (1) EP positive — a date exactly >7 days out with valid 2FA succeeds; (2) NEG negative — a date in the past is rejected with HTTP 422; (3) BVA boundary — a date exactly 7 days from now (inclusive lower bound) is accepted; (4) BVA boundary — a date exactly 6 days out (one day inside the forbidden window) is rejected; (5) DT decision — valid date but missing/failed 2FA is rejected, exercising the authentication branch independently of the date rule.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_with_2fa_succeeds
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 14 days (well inside valid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at and valid 2FA token
expected: Participants receive notification (notification flag in response or side-effect check)
trace: R-WEB-096: review scheduled with date >= now+7d
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

def test_schedule_review_valid_date_with_2fa_succeeds(
    manager_api_client,   # <fixture> authenticated httpx client with role:manager
    valid_reviewee_id,    # <fixture> returns an existing reviewee user id
    totp_token,           # <fixture> returns current TOTP code for manager 2FA
):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": valid_reviewee_id,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
        "two_fa_token": totp_token,
    }
    response = manager_api_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain review id"
    assert body.get("notifications_sent") is True, "Participants must be notified"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected_with_422
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now - 1 day (past date, invalid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at and valid 2FA token
expected: Error message references scheduled_at validation failure
trace: R-WEB-096: past dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-096: error pathway — 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected_with_422(
    manager_api_client,
    valid_reviewee_id,
    totp_token,
):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": valid_reviewee_id,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
        "two_fa_token": totp_token,
    }
    response = manager_api_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    errors = body.get("detail", body.get("errors", ""))
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(errors), "Error must reference scheduled_at field"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_out_accepted
preconditions: System clock is stable (no DST transition within test window)
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + exactly 7 days (lower valid boundary)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at and valid 2FA token
expected: Review is persisted with scheduled_at = now+7d
trace: R-WEB-096: at least 7 days in advance — boundary at exactly 7d must be accepted

tests.name: test_schedule_review_6_days_out_rejected_with_422
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 6 days 23 hours 59 minutes (one minute below 7-day boundary)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at and valid 2FA token
expected: Error references scheduled_at must be at least 7 days in the future
trace: R-WEB-096: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-096: BVA upper invalid boundary — just below the 7-day threshold
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

def test_schedule_review_6_days_out_rejected_with_422(
    manager_api_client,
    valid_reviewee_id,
    totp_token,
):
    scheduled_at = (
        datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=6, hours=23, minutes=59)
    ).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": valid_reviewee_id,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
        "two_fa_token": totp_token,
    }
    response = manager_api_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422 for sub-7-day date, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body.get("detail", body.get("errors", "")))

tests.name: test_schedule_review_invalid_2fa_token_rejected
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 14 days (valid date partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with valid date but wrong 2FA token
trace: R-WEB-096: action requires two-factor authentication confirmation
R-WEB-096: DT — valid date AND invalid 2FA => rejection
R-WEB-096: additional variant semantics — 2FA is a hard gate
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

INVALID_2FA_TOKEN = "000000"  # statistically invalid TOTP value

def test_schedule_review_invalid_2fa_token_rejected(
    manager_api_client,
    valid_reviewee_id,
):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": valid_reviewee_id,
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
        "two_fa_token": INVALID_2FA_TOKEN,
    }
    response = manager_api_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected 401/403 for bad 2FA, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    detail = str(body.get("detail", body.get("errors", ""))).lower()
    assert any(kw in detail for kw in ("2fa", "mfa", "token", "authentication")), (
        "Error body must reference 2FA failure"
    )
